Respect overdue for 'Mr. V,' inventor of figure skating's short program   WEEKEND READ | A fixture at Montreal arenas for decades, coach Karel Vosátka left a big mark on the sport. The problem is no one knew what he’d done.

The best place for a picnic is wherever you decide to have a picnic
Juraj Slafkovsky impressed Canadiens both on and off the ice   No. 1 pick at NHL Draft isn't your typical teenager — and it's not just because he's 6-foot-4 and 229 pounds.

Gallery: Pop-culture fans dress for Montreal Comiccon   After a pandemic break, Montreal Comiccon, which usually attracts in the neighbourhood of 60,000 visitors, returns to the Palais des congrès July 8-10.
Rogers services down for thousands of customers across Canada   Highest concentration in Ontario, but reports from across country as well.
Montreal jazz fest daily picks: July 9, 2022   Shane Murphy will help close down the jazz fest with three sets backed by a full band.
Montreal weather: A gorgeous weekend on tap   It doesn't get much better than this: sunny skies, low humidity and comfortable temperatures.

Brownstein: Just for Laughs welcomes global talent back to 'summer camp for comics'   The Montreal comedy festival is making up for lost time after two-plus years of the pandemic, presenting nearly 300 shows — 75 of them free — from July 13 to 31.

Robert Libman: Subplots add drama to October's Quebec election   While many see a CAQ majority as a foregone conclusion, the Duhaime factor and new rights-focused parties make things less predictable.

Brownstein: Shane Murphy's wildfire blues will blaze at Montreal jazz fest   “Everybody had a hard time during the pandemic. I just thought I would take those feelings of darkness and forlornness I had, and it all came together,” he says of his new album, Get Religion.
Editorial: Quebec's COVID plan is everyone for themselves, it seems   The situation is "under control," Health Minister Christian Dubé indicated on Thursday. Is it really? And if so, for how long?
Fariha Naqvi-Mohamed: This Eid will be special, for a few reasons   A sense of community is essential for everyone and it is something I have sorely missed, as have my children.

Stu Cowan: Canadiens GM Kent Hughes puts his plan into action   He's a confident man who is very comfortable in his own skin and with whatever decisions he decides to make. Time will tell if he's right.
Canadiens aim for flash and substance on Day 2 of NHL draft   Undersized defenceman Lane Hutson, chosen 62nd, could be a steal (or a wasted pick).

Son of Canadiens GM Kent Hughes drafted in second round by L.A. Kings   “He’s taught me everything I know about hockey and I think a strong suit of my game is my hockey sense," Jack Hughes says about his father.
Kirby Dach looking forward to a fresh start with the Canadiens   Chicago Blackhawks selected the 6-foot-4, 197-pound centre with the No. 3 overall pick at the 2019 NHL Draft.

'It's a great day for Slovakia,' says Juraj Slafkovsky, Montreal's top draft pick   Slafkovsky became the highest drafted player from his country when the Canadiens selected him as their No. 1 choice on Thursday.
Quebec employers group wants older workers to stay on the job   The Conseil du patronat is offering companies help with best practices to attract or retain experienced workers.
Canada's jobless rate drops to record low of 4.9%: What you need to know   Kevin Carmichael: It feels like Canada is entering a new phase of persistently strong demand for workers

Montreal businesses, customers have no plans to return to COVID-19 restrictions   Businesses and their customers say they have no intention on returning to masks and restrictions in the wake of news that the province has entered a seventh wave.

Péladeau family quarrel over inheritance ends as high court rejects brothers' appeal   Anne-Marie Péladeau had claimed $88 million in the dispute, which dates back to a settlement reached in 2000, three years after Pierre Péladeau died.

Passengers, advocates cry foul on Air Canada compensation   Despite thousands of scrapped flights and late arrivals, customers are struggling to file successful complaints or learn the nuances of complex regulations amid the overwhelming surge in summer travel.
Dunlevy: Goodbye jazz fest, hello Nuits d'Afrique   Featuring 150 events and activities, including 70 performances by artists from 30 countries, the festival puts the “world” in world music.
Montreal circus fest is happy to be back and soaring to new heights   The festival will include shows around three giants in public spaces around downtown.
Suuns bring their experimental rock to the street for Montreal jazz fest   "If it's honest and it's good ... people will connect with it on some level," lead singer Ben Shemie says.

Bran Van 3000 in control of the party as it prepares for Montreal jazz fest show   “I think we’re all blown away by how easy and smooth it is,” ringleader James Di Salvio said of the Montreal collective picking up where it left off.

Montreal exhibit by Yayoi Kusama, 93, invites us to contemplate universe   The Japanese artist is best known for her Infinity Mirrored Rooms, in which solitary viewers are plunked into an expansive disco cosmos.
Bill Zacharkiw's Wines of the Week: July 8, 2022   Every week, Bill Zacharkiw identifies his top wine picks available at the SAQ and offers ideas for food pairings.

Six O’Clock Solution: Mix it up with sticky honey salmon   The recipe comes with variations on the vegetables — asparagus and peas can be replaced with string beans and kale — from an easygoing cookbook.

Could a Roe v. Wade-style reversal of abortion rights happen in Canada?   Prior attempts to make abortion illegal have failed — but abortion access and misinformation are still big problems in Canada.

Here are 5 cool new things to try at SÉPAQ parks this summer   Discover what the glaciers left behind. Count the stars at a Dark Sky Reserve. Or pit yourself against your siblings at a ninja-style obstacle course.
Here's how you and the kids can have a busy and happy summer vacation   The school-year routine makes our children feel secure. But when summer vacation arrives, the entire family must readapt.
True Crime Byline: We should have seen JoAnn Wilson's murder coming   Leader-Post city editor Barb Pacholik recalls the many turns of the Colin Thatcher case across decades.

True Crime Byline: How one childhood incident pushed Dawn Crey into a downward spiral from which she would never escape   The new podcast True Crime Byline looks back at The Vancouver Sun investigation into Vancouver's missing women, a case that eventually led to the arrest and conviction of Robert Pickton. This profile of missing woman Dawn Crey was part of a series of investigative stories published in 2001.

True Crime Byline: A Politician Murdered his Ex Wife. We Should Have Seen it Coming   Looking back on the murder of JoAnn Wilson today, what's most surprising is that we were surprised it happened at all.

True Crime Byline podcast: The Article that Blew Up a Murder Trial   Listen to this podcast episode for the shocking story of how a National Post article about a teen vampire led to a mistrial, as told by reporter Joseph Brean

Douglas Todd: Canadian cops far less likely to kill, or be killed, than U.S. counterparts   Analysis: Canadian police are often accused of using excessive force, but a U.S. officer is five times more likely than a Canadian cop to shoot and kill.
News Roundup: A Canadian bomb-proof Toyota, and a Porsche-powered VW van   Plus: video footage of a 15-year-old off-roader flipping a Jeep off a cliff—don't worry, it ends well
I'd rather watch Nissans race than Ferraris—here's why   The Nissan Sentra Cup was one of the highlights of the Canadian Grand Prix weekend in Montreal, and it's for a good reason

Collector Classics: 1962 Chevrolet Corvette convertible   It was a leap of faith that couples sometimes need to make. He saw a big withdrawal from their joint savings account. She would only say it was a good investment and he needed to trust her judgment. She added that their financial advisor was in favour of the expense.
2023 Toyota Crown teaser hints at sedan-style SUV   The patent images being passed around now seem legit, with the new SUV to be properly revealed on July 15
GMC announces 2023 Sierra 1500 AT4X AEV Edition   Factory-installed heavy-duty parts will make it the most capable off-road Sierra yet

'ALS will be a treatable chronic disease': New treatment slows disease progression and increases life expectancy   McGill University's Dr. Angela Genge is optimistic about what the future holds for amyotrophic lateral sclerosis care: "Every step gives our patients more time, better function for longer, better care every year."
Asking For A Friend: Are essential oils worth the hype?   The oils from plants offer natural healing and cleansing benefits, but they can also be dangerous.
Our friends may be our friends because of the way they smell   When it comes to judging other people on their body odour, we may be more like other mammals than we realize, according to a new study.
TikTok Tuesday: #healthycoke — a trend that’s not so healthy for your teeth (WATCH)   It may be low in sugar, but carbonated water mixed with vinegar is a terrible choice for your teeth.
Putting our phones on silent mode only makes them more tempting, study says   Blame FOMO for how hard it can be for us to stop looking at our phones all the time.
Ontario police seize cannabis worth $61 million   Estimated 45,000-plus illegal pot plants and eight metric tonnes of processed weed confiscated.

Joe Rogan responds to border drug seizure: ‘You should take all these drugs and rethink your life’   Border agents seized small amounts of ecstasy, cannabis and psilocybin mushrooms from a vehicle entering New York.
One of Canada's biggest universities will have a pot store on campus   The planned store would represent the pot company Burb's eighth and final one allowed in the province under its retail cap.
Ontario casinos laundered $3 million in illegal cannabis proceeds: RCMP   Weed worth approx. $24.8 million confiscated.

Man's ploy to flush over 20 kilograms of cannabis down the toilet leads him straight to jail   Officers also found 21 kilograms of weed, additional bags of prepackaged marijuana, a handgun and over $16K in cash.
